message:
fix(func,core,vdbe): fix printf NULL handling + ALTER TABLE row padding

Two fixes bringing conformance from 932→934 pass:

1. printf('%q', NULL) now returns '(NULL)' (was empty string)
   printf('%s', NULL) now returns '' (was '(null)')
   Both match SQLite's documented behavior.

2. ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN now pads existing MemDB rows with the new
   column's DEFAULT value via MemTable::pad_rows_to_column_count().
   Previously, pre-existing rows had fewer columns than the updated
   schema expected, causing out-of-range column accesses to return NULL.

   Note: The ALTER TABLE tests still fail due to a deeper column-ordering
   bug where the MemDB reload after ALTER TABLE assigns columns to wrong
   positions. This padding fix is necessary but not sufficient.

Conformance: 932→934 pass, 6→4 fail.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
